title: Data Needed to Compute System Response
summary: This page introduces the concept of data required to compute system response, using a simple RC circuit with a current source as an example. It derives the output voltage equation showing that the response can be computed either from the entire past input history or from an initial condition (capacitor voltage) combined with the input from some starting time t0 onward. The initial capacitor voltage vC(t0) encapsulates all relevant past information about the circuit.

figures.caption: An RC circuit driven by a current source x(t) with resistor R and capacitor C in parallel, where the capacitor voltage v_c(t) serves as the state variable and y(t) is the output voltage.
